chat.ready: ARIA is ready. Type your first message.
chat.long_running_skill: A longer-running skill is still executing. This can take 30-60 seconds...
chat.clear_confirm: Clear chat history in this browser?
chat.tool_create_connection_hint: Creates a simple connection via chat with a confirm step.
chat.tool_update_connection_hint: Updates simple connections or metadata only via chat.
chat.tool_delete_connection_hint: Deletes a connection profile with a confirm step.

login.title: Login
login.subtitle: Sign in with username and password.
login.bootstrap_hint: No user exists yet. Please create the first user here. This first user will automatically become admin.
login.bootstrap_admin_hint: After that you can configure LLMs, embeddings, connections, skills, and security in admin mode. The regular user mode intentionally stays a reduced day-to-day workspace.
login.normal_signin: Regular sign-in with an existing account.
login.mode_hint: If advanced configuration areas are missing: sign in as admin and enable admin mode under Users.
login.bootstrap_locked: Bootstrap is currently locked. Please ask an existing admin to unlock it.
login.password_confirm_hint: For safety, enter the same password a second time.
login.cache_hint: If colors or layout still look old: do one hard reload.

config_language.title: Language
config_language.subtitle: Set default language and manage language files.

config.h1_desc: Configure how ARIA thinks, responds, and computes.
config.llm_desc: Provider, API base, API key, model, temperature, tokens, timeout.
config.embed_desc: Separate model for memory, search, and similarity.
config.memory_desc: So ARIA keeps important facts and returns stronger recall results.
config.memory_routing_desc: Adjust keywords like “remember this” and “do you remember”.
config.skill_routing_desc: Central trigger map for all custom skills with direct JSON linkage.
config.persona_desc: Define ARIA tone, character, and working style.
config.prompt_desc: Edit persona and skill prompts directly in the browser.
config.connections_desc: Everything for external systems and access.
config.connections_ssh_desc: Hosts, users, keys, and allowed commands for runtime skills.
config.connections_hub_desc: SSH, Discord, SFTP and future connection types each on their own page.
config.logs_desc: Automatically clean up token/run logs and control retention.
config.workbench_desc: For debugging, fine-tuning, and “I know what I am doing” moments.
config.security_desc: Control bootstrap lock and login protection centrally.
config.files_desc: Direct access to relevant text files without an IDE.
config.error_interpreter_desc: Rules that translate technical SSH and package errors into clearer user-facing messages.
config.language_desc: Set default language and edit language files in Advanced mode.

config_skill_routing.title: Skill Routing
config_skill_routing.subtitle: Central trigger overview for all custom skills. One trigger can map to one or more skills.
config_skill_routing.load_errors: Some skill manifests could not be loaded:
config_skill_routing.suggest_all: Suggest keywords for all skills via LLM
config_skill_routing.info_suggest_all: LLM suggestion applied: {updated} skills updated, {total} keywords generated.
config_skill_routing.info_suggest_one: LLM suggestion applied for {skill}: {total} keywords generated.

config_llm.title: Chat LLM Configuration
config_llm.subtitle: LLM parameters are editable; sensitive fields (API key) are stored in the secure database.
config_llm.save_help: Save updates the active profile or creates a new one if you enter a different profile name below. "Save as profile" does the same thing explicitly.
config_llm.profile_name_hint: Example: litellm-main. If you enter a different name here, the normal Save button will also create or overwrite that profile.
config_llm.js_models_loaded: Models loaded. Selection will update the model value.

config_embed.title: Embeddings Configuration
config_embed.subtitle: Embedding model configuration (memory & similarity). API keys are stored in the secure database.
config_embed.save_help: Save updates the active profile or creates a new one if you enter a different profile name below. "Save as profile" does the same thing explicitly.
config_embed.profile_name_hint: Example: litellm-embeddings. If you enter a different name here, the normal Save button will also create or overwrite that profile.
config_embed.js_models_loaded: Models loaded. Selection will update the model value.
config_embed.memory_guard_body: Changing the embedding model or API base can make existing memory and RAG unreliable until it has been re-indexed.
config_embed.confirm_checkbox: I understand that changing embeddings can make existing memory and RAG unusable until it is re-indexed.
config_embed.confirm_phrase_hint: Only needed if you really switch to a different embedding fingerprint. Phrase:

config_routing.title: Routing & Memory Rules
config_routing.subtitle: Plain-text rules for router and memory extraction. One line = one entry.
config_routing.scope_hint: Default is fallback. Language scope overrides only that language.
config_routing.index_subtitle: Admin/debug index for future Qdrant routing. Not live in chat yet.
config_routing.live_enable_label: Enable live Qdrant routing in chat
config_routing.live_ask_label: Ask instead of falling back when Qdrant is unsure
config_routing.testbench_subtitle: Dry-run: shows how ARIA would resolve a target. Nothing is executed.
config_routing.test_no_execution: Dry-run only: no command, message, feed or file action was executed.
config_routing.test_no_match_hint: Rebuild the index or improve title, description, aliases and tags on the connection profile.
config_routing.test_deterministic_hint: No exact profile name or alias matched.
config_routing.test_qdrant_hint: Accepted / returned candidates from the generated routing collection.

config_prompts.title: Prompt Editing
config_prompts.subtitle: Edit relevant prompt files only. No Python code on this page.

config_conn.title: Connections
config_conn.subtitle: Clear, simple connection profiles for external systems and skills.
config_conn.delete_profile_hint: Deletes the currently loaded profile including secure-store secrets and health status.
config_conn.delete_profile_none: Load an existing profile first if you want to delete it.
config_conn.delete_profile_ssh_hint: Local SSH key files are intentionally kept.
config_conn.manage_existing_subtitle: Choose an existing profile, update it deliberately, and remove it when needed.
config_conn.create_new_subtitle: Use this area for a deliberately new connection. The fields start empty and create a new profile.
config_conn.current_profile_loaded_hint: You are editing this existing profile. Changes apply to this exact entry.
config_conn.no_profile_loaded_hint: Choose a profile above to edit or delete it.
config_conn.new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new profile name below and save.
config_conn.test_hint: Tests passwordless SSH login with configured key and returns clear feedback.
config_conn.step1_subtitle: Default: Save directly runs key exchange if you provide a password.
config_conn.login_user: Login user for key exchange (optional, else profile user)
config_conn.password_hint: Without password only the profile is saved. ARIA still runs the automatic connection test and shows the result above.
config_conn.run_key_exchange: Run key exchange directly after save (default)
config_conn.allow_commands_hint: One line per command. Empty = no permission for
config_conn.tools_subtitle: Only needed if you want to run key exchange later as a separate step.
config_conn.public_key_empty: No public key found for this profile yet.
config_conn.summary_active_hint: Current profile for editing and details.
config_conn.summary_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the SSH check.
config_conn.profile_center_subtitle: Load an existing profile or create a new one. Save is the main action; with a password, key exchange runs right away.
config_conn.recommended_flow_text: 1) Save profile with host/user. 2) Provide one-time login password for key exchange if needed. 3) Save. 4) ARIA tests the connection automatically.
config_conn.ssh_service_url_hint: Optional: web UI or product page for the service on this host. ARIA can use it to suggest description, tags, and routing aliases.
config_conn.ssh_create_matching_sftp: Also create a matching SFTP profile
config_conn.ssh_create_matching_sftp_hint: Creates an additional SFTP profile on save with host, user, port, and SSH key. If the ref ends with -ssh, ARIA automatically turns it into -sftp.
config_conn.live_status_hint: ARIA checks all SSH profiles when this page opens and again right after saving a profile.
config_conn.no_profiles_status_hint: No SSH profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.discord_subtitle: Discord webhooks for notifications and skill output.
config_conn.discord_profiles_hint: Available Discord webhook profiles in ARIA.
config_conn.discord_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the Discord check.
config_conn.discord_webhook_hint: The webhook URL is stored in the secure store, not in config.yaml.
config_conn.discord_live_status: Live status of all Discord profiles
config_conn.discord_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all Discord profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.discord_no_profiles_hint: No Discord profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.discord_profile_center_subtitle: Choose an existing Discord profile or create a new one. Saving tests the webhook automatically.
config_conn.discord_edit_profile_hint: Choose an existing profile and load it. Saving below updates that exact profile. For a new one, simply enter a new profile name below.
config_conn.discord_new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new profile name below and save.
config_conn.discord_recommended_flow_text: 1) Create a webhook in the target Discord channel. 2) Paste the full webhook URL here and save. 3) ARIA stores the URL in the secure store. 4) ARIA tests the webhook automatically.
config_conn.discord_password_hint: The webhook URL is stored in the secure store and never written into config.yaml. Leave it empty to keep the existing secret.
config_conn.discord_alerting_hint: Here you control whether ARIA may send visible test posts and whether skills may actively use this profile as a Discord target.
config_conn.discord_send_test_messages: Send nerdy test message to Discord
config_conn.discord_send_test_messages_hint: When enabled, ARIA sends a visible A.R.I.A handshake message during connection tests. When disabled, ARIA validates the webhook silently without a test post.
config_conn.discord_allow_skill_messages: Allow skill messages via this profile
config_conn.discord_allow_skill_messages_hint: When disabled, skills cannot use this Discord profile as a `discord_send` target.
config_conn.discord_event_routing_hint: These categories turn Discord into a small alarm and log hub for ARIA. Only important events are sent, not complete raw logs.
config_conn.discord_alert_skill_errors_hint: Sends a message to Discord when a skill run fails.
config_conn.discord_alert_safe_fix_hint: Sends messages when a safe-fix is ready or has been executed.
config_conn.discord_alert_connection_changes_hint: Sends a message when a configured connection switches from green to red or back again.
config_conn.discord_alert_system_events_hint: Sends compact messages on ARIA startup and similar system events.
config_conn.discord_active_routes_hint: This gives you a quick overview of which Discord functions are currently enabled for this profile.
config_conn.discord_active_routes_empty: No Discord routes are active right now.
config_conn.sftp_subtitle: SFTP profiles for file access and transfer.
config_conn.sftp_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the SFTP check.
config_conn.sftp_password_hint: The password is stored in the secure store, not in config.yaml.
config_conn.sftp_key_hint: SFTP can use the configured SSH key directly.
config_conn.sftp_live_status: Live status of all SFTP profiles
config_conn.sftp_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all SFTP profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.sftp_no_profiles_hint: No SFTP profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.sftp_profile_center_subtitle: Choose an existing SFTP profile or create a new one. Saving tests the connection automatically.
config_conn.sftp_import_ssh_hint: Copies host, user, port, and key path from an existing SSH profile. Root path and password remain SFTP-specific.
config_conn.sftp_new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new profile name below and save.
config_conn.sftp_recommended_flow_text: 1) Optionally import an SSH profile as a starting point. 2) Save the SFTP profile with host/user and password or key. 3) Set the root path. 4) Save. 5) ARIA tests the connection automatically.
config_conn.sftp_key_path_hint: When set, SFTP uses this key instead of a password. Ideal for profiles copied from SSH.
config_conn.sftp_password_placeholder: Leave empty to keep the existing password
config_conn.sftp_password_store_hint: The password is stored in the secure store and never written into config.yaml.
config_conn.hub_hint: Each connection type has its own page so configuration stays clean, modular, and easy to extend later.
config_conn.smb_subtitle: SMB shares for Windows and NAS file access.
config_conn.smb_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the SMB check.
config_conn.smb_password_hint: Password is stored in the secure store, not in config.yaml.
config_conn.smb_live_status: Live status of all SMB profiles
config_conn.smb_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all SMB profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.smb_no_profiles_hint: No SMB profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.smb_profile_center_subtitle: Choose an existing SMB profile or create a new one. Saving tests the connection automatically.
config_conn.smb_new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new profile name below and save.
config_conn.smb_recommended_flow_text: 1) Save SMB profile with host, share, user and password. 2) ARIA stores the password in the secure store. 3) Save. 4) ARIA tests the connection automatically.
config_conn.smb_password_placeholder: Leave empty to keep the existing password
config_conn.smb_password_store_hint: The password is stored in the secure store and never written into config.yaml.
config_conn.webhook_subtitle: Generic outgoing webhooks for skills and notifications.
config_conn.webhook_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the webhook check.
config_conn.webhook_secret_hint: The webhook URL is stored in the secure store, not in config.yaml.
config_conn.webhook_live_status: Live status of all webhook profiles
config_conn.webhook_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all webhook profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.webhook_no_profiles_hint: No webhook profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.webhook_profile_center_subtitle: Choose an existing webhook profile or create a new one. Saving tests the endpoint automatically.
config_conn.webhook_new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new profile name below and save.
config_conn.webhook_recommended_flow_text: 1) Save webhook profile with URL. 2) ARIA stores the URL in the secure store. 3) Save. 4) ARIA tests the endpoint automatically.
config_conn.webhook_url_hint: The webhook URL is stored in the secure store and never written into config.yaml. Leave it empty to keep the existing secret.
config_conn.email_subtitle: SMTP profiles for mail delivery, reports and alerting.
config_conn.email_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the SMTP check.
config_conn.email_password_hint: SMTP password is stored in the secure store, not in config.yaml.
config_conn.email_live_status: Live status of all SMTP profiles
config_conn.email_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all SMTP profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.email_no_profiles_hint: No SMTP profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.email_profile_center_subtitle: Choose an existing SMTP profile or create a new one. Saving tests the SMTP connection automatically.
config_conn.email_new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new SMTP profile name below and save.
config_conn.email_recommended_flow_text: 1) Save SMTP profile with host, user and password. 2) ARIA stores the password in the secure store. 3) Save. 4) ARIA tests the SMTP connection automatically.
config_conn.email_password_placeholder: Leave empty to keep the existing SMTP password
config_conn.email_password_store_hint: The password is stored in the secure store and never written into config.yaml.
config_conn.http_api_subtitle: Authenticated REST endpoints for later API-driven skills.
config_conn.http_api_profiles_hint: Available HTTP API profiles in ARIA.
config_conn.http_api_healthy_hint: Profiles with successful HTTP API checks.
config_conn.http_api_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the HTTP API check.
config_conn.http_api_token_hint: Bearer token is stored in the secure store when provided.
config_conn.http_api_live_status: Live status of all HTTP API profiles
config_conn.http_api_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all HTTP API profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.http_api_no_profiles_hint: No HTTP API profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.http_api_profile_center_subtitle: Choose an existing HTTP API profile or create a new one. Saving tests the endpoint automatically.
config_conn.http_api_new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new profile name below and save.
config_conn.http_api_recommended_flow_text: 1) Save HTTP API profile with base URL and optional token. 2) ARIA stores the token in the secure store. 3) Save. 4) ARIA tests the endpoint automatically.
config_conn.http_api_token_placeholder: Leave empty to keep the existing token
config_conn.http_api_token_store_hint: The token is stored in the secure store and never written into config.yaml.
config_conn.rss_subtitle: RSS and Atom feeds as lightweight read-only sources.
config_conn.rss_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the RSS check.
config_conn.rss_feed_hint: Feed URL is stored directly in config.yaml.
config_conn.rss_live_status: Live status of all RSS profiles
config_conn.rss_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all RSS profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.rss_no_profiles_hint: No RSS profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.rss_profile_center_subtitle: Choose an existing RSS profile or create a new one. Saving tests the feed automatically.
config_conn.rss_new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new profile name below and save.
config_conn.editing_profile_hint: You are editing this existing profile. Use “New profile” above for an empty form.
config_conn.creating_profile_hint: The fields are intentionally empty. Save to create a new RSS profile, or load an existing one above.
config_conn.rss_recommended_flow_text: 1) Save RSS profile with feed URL. 2) Save. 3) ARIA tests the feed automatically.
config_conn.rss_group_name_hint: Manually assigned groups stay unchanged during LLM refresh. Leave empty if ARIA may auto-group this feed.
config_conn.rss_search_placeholder: Search only RSS title, URL, ref, group, and tags
config_conn.rss_global_poll_hint: This interval applies to all RSS feeds, so you do not have to update each profile manually when you have many feeds.
config_conn.rss_opml_hint: Import many feeds from an OPML file or export your current RSS collection back to OPML.
config_conn.mqtt_subtitle: MQTT brokers for events, IoT and message routing.
config_conn.mqtt_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the MQTT check.
config_conn.mqtt_password_hint: Password is stored in the secure store, not in config.yaml.
config_conn.mqtt_live_status: Live status of all MQTT profiles
config_conn.mqtt_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all MQTT profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.mqtt_no_profiles_hint: No MQTT profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.mqtt_profile_center_subtitle: Choose an existing MQTT profile or create a new one. Saving tests the broker automatically.
config_conn.mqtt_new_profile_hint: New profile: enter a new profile name below and save.
config_conn.mqtt_recommended_flow_text: 1) Save MQTT profile with host, user and password. 2) ARIA stores the password in the secure store. 3) Save. 4) ARIA tests the broker automatically.
config_conn.mqtt_password_placeholder: Leave empty to keep the existing password
config_conn.mqtt_password_store_hint: The password is stored in the secure store and never written into config.yaml.
config_conn.imap_subtitle: IMAP profiles for mailbox access and inbound mail.
config_conn.imap_issue_hint: Profiles that currently fail the IMAP check.
config_conn.imap_password_hint: IMAP password is stored in the secure store, not in config.yaml.
config_conn.imap_live_status: Live status of all IMAP profiles
config_conn.imap_live_status_hint: ARIA checks all IMAP profiles when this page opens and right after saving a profile.
config_conn.imap_no_profiles_hint: No IMAP profiles yet. Save a profile and ARIA will test it automatically.
config_conn.imap_recommended_flow_text: 1) Save IMAP profile with host, user and password. 2) ARIA stores the password in the secure store. 3) Save. 4) ARIA tests the mailbox connection automatically.
config_conn.imap_password_placeholder: Leave empty to keep the existing IMAP password
config_conn.rss_groups_hint: ARIA groups RSS profiles by theme. Manually assigned groups stay fixed; LLM refresh only re-sorts feeds without a group.
config_conn.meta_hint: These values help ARIA recognize connections more naturally and make later modular extensions easier.
config_conn.meta_aliases_hint: Separate multiple values with commas or line breaks. ARIA uses these terms for more natural chat matching.

config_security.title: Security Guardrails
config_security.subtitle: Control login security and reusable guardrail profiles for ARIA modules.
config_security.bootstrap_section_hint: Baseline safeguards for first-login behavior and the security store.
config_security.bootstrap_explainer: As long as no user exists, ARIA can create the first login as a bootstrap admin. After initial setup, bootstrap should stay locked so an empty or broken user DB cannot accidentally create a new admin again.
config_security.when_on: When enabled: with empty user DB,
config_security.auto_admin_created: automatic admin is created via login.
config_security.when_off: When disabled: first login on empty user DB creates an admin automatically.
config_security.guardrails_subtitle: Reusable safety profiles for specific services. Guardrails can be attached to compatible connections and extended modularly over time.
config_security.guardrail_description_placeholder: Allows only harmless read operations on Linux hosts.
config_security.guardrail_allow_hint: If set, at least one entry must appear in the request before ARIA may execute it.
config_security.guardrail_deny_hint: If an entry appears, ARIA blocks the request immediately.
config_security.delete_guardrail_hint: Deletes the profile and removes the assignment from compatible connections that used it.
config_security.create_guardrail_hint: A guardrail is a reusable safety profile for a specific service type. Afterwards you can attach it to compatible connections.
config_security.guardrail_kind_hint: The type defines which connection classes may use this guardrail later.
config_security.http_guardrail_hint: HTTP guardrails inspect target URL, method, and relevant request fragments before ARIA actually sends the request.
config_security.file_guardrail_hint: File guardrails inspect operation and path before ARIA reads, writes, or lists directories.
config_security.sample_guardrails_subtitle: Starter packs from /app/samples/security that can be imported directly into security.guardrails.
config_security.sample_guardrails_empty_desc: No samples/security directory was found in the container.

config_files.title: File Editor
config_files.subtitle: Edit only approved user files and view internal help texts safely in the UI.
config_files.readonly_hint: This file is read only in the UI so support-relevant help texts cannot be changed accidentally.
config_files.save_disabled: Saving is disabled for this file.

config_logs.title: Logs & Retention
config_logs.subtitle: Control how long token/run logs for stats and activities are kept.
config_logs.retention_hint: 0 = never auto-delete. Recommended for normal operation: 14-90 days.
config_logs.reset_subtitle: Deletes the stored token/run history for stats and activities. Memories and connection profiles stay untouched.
config_logs.reset_confirm_hint: Type RESET to confirm the reset deliberately.
config_logs.factory_reset_subtitle: Deletes user data, memories, connections, guardrails, custom skills, chat history, SSH keys, and local runtime caches. Runtime secrets and prompts remain intact.
config_logs.factory_reset_confirm_hint: Type FACTORY RESET to wipe this ARIA instance deliberately.

config_error_interpreter.title: Error Interpreter
config_error_interpreter.subtitle: Rules for SSH, apt, sudo, and runtime failures. ARIA uses these rules to turn raw technical errors into clearer user-facing messages.

config_users.title: Team & Access
config_users.subtitle: Create users and manage roles. Leave password empty to keep it unchanged.
config_users.admin_toggle_hint: Shows technical UI details (debug view) for admins only.
config_users.admin_mode_explainer: Admin mode reveals deeper system areas such as LLM, embeddings, security, logs, and the file editor. User mode intentionally stays leaner for daily use. Role permissions and admin mode are separate concepts: only admins can use this toggle at all.
config_users.create_user_hint: New users start with the selected role. For the current ALPHA, ARIA is still primarily designed as a personal single-user system; shared ownership/RBAC is a later architecture step.

stats.health_subtitle: Lightweight runtime health of the main ARIA services.
stats.connections_live_title: Live status of all configured connections
stats.connections_live_hint: ARIA checks all configured connections here and refreshes the status when the page opens.
stats.connections_live_empty: No connections configured yet. Once profiles are saved, ARIA shows them here with live status.
stats.health_overall_warn: At least one service needs attention.
stats.health_overall_error: At least one service currently reports an error.
stats.rss_summary_ok_hint: RSS feeds with successful fetch status.
stats.rss_summary_issue_hint: RSS feeds currently reporting an error.

activities.title: Activities & Runs
activities.subtitle: Operational skill runs and system actions from the logs, kept separate from semantic memory.

activities.title: Activities & Runs
activities.subtitle: Operational skill runs and system actions from the logs, kept separate from semantic memory.

memories.create_subtitle: Add an entry deliberately. ARIA stores it in the matching memory collection.
memories.create_placeholder: e.g. I usually use my main system for server files.
memories.upload_subtitle: Upload text files or PDFs with embedded text into an existing or new Qdrant collection. RAG v1 supports TXT, Markdown, and text PDFs.
memories.upload_collection_hint: If nothing is selected, ARIA uses the active document collection or creates the personal default document collection.
memories.upload_button_loading: Document is being chunked and imported ...
memories.upload_progress: The document is being prepared, chunked, and imported into Qdrant. Depending on the PDF, this may take a moment.
memories.documents_subtitle: Imported documents are grouped here by filename instead of appearing only as individual chunks.
memories.documents_collections_subtitle: Each tile represents a document collection. Expand it to see the documents inside.
memories.documents_in_store_subtitle: Click a document first. ARIA then shows the chunks that belong to exactly this store.
memories.collections_subtitle: Open a collection in the Memory view to inspect its entries directly.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Enable ARIA capabilities here. New skills appear automatically from JSON manifests.
skills.load_errors: Some skill files could not be loaded:
skills.core_memory_desc: Stores and recalls knowledge via Qdrant.
skills.core_auto_memory_desc: Automatic fact extraction without code words.
skills.none_text: Create your first skill with the wizard or import a JSON manifest.
skills.wizard_auto_keywords: Auto-generate trigger keywords via LLM when empty

memories_map.none_for_user: No collections currently exist for this user.
memories_map.routing_collections_subtitle: Generated Qdrant collections for routing and system logic. They are not part of semantic user memory, but are shown here for completeness.
memories_map.rollups_subtitle: This shows condensed daily context as weekly and monthly rollups.
memories_map.graph_subtitle: A simple overview of this user's memory types, collections, documents, and rollups.

config_memory.setup_subtitle: Without selection, the default user collection is used automatically:
config_memory.secret_leave_unchanged: Leave empty to keep the securely stored key unchanged.
config_memory.secret_optional_hint: Only needed when this Qdrant instance is protected by an API key.
config_memory.qdrant_admin_only: Qdrant link visible in admin mode only (On).
config_memory.qdrant_storage_warning: Qdrant storage and API are currently out of sync
config_memory.new_collection_hint: Will be created in Qdrant on first store with matching embedding dimension.
config_memory.no_collections_found: No collections found in this Qdrant instance yet.
config_memory.rollup_subtitle_delete: and deletes the old day collections afterwards.
config_memory.summary_prompt_hint: Prompt text is managed in Prompt Studio, not as a file path in end-user UI.
config_memory.compress_after_days: Move day context to rollup after how many days?
config_memory.monthly_after_days: Create monthly summary after how many days?
config_memory.move_now_hint: Processes only day-context collections older than the configured threshold.
config_memory.auto_subtitle: Extracts facts without code words and uses session + user memory during recall.
config_memory.max_facts_hint: Tip: keep this small (e.g. 2-4) to keep prompt context lean.

stats.preflight_subtitle: Checks from the latest startup/runtime diagnostic run.
stats.preflight_warn: At least one startup check needs attention.
stats.preflight_error: At least one startup check currently fails.
stats.health_overall_warn: At least one status check needs attention.
stats.health_overall_error: At least one service currently reports an error.
base.subtitle: Adaptive Reasoning & Intelligence Agent
config.h1_desc: Configure how ARIA thinks, responds, and computes.
config.llm_desc: Provider, API base, API key, model, temperature, tokens, and timeout.
config.embed_desc: Separate model for memory, search, and similarity.
config.memory_desc: So ARIA keeps important facts and returns stronger recall results.
config.memory_routing_desc: Adjust keywords like “remember this” and “do you remember”.
config.skill_routing_desc: Central trigger map for all custom skills with direct JSON linkage.
config.persona_desc: Define ARIA tone, prompt style, visual identity, and language.
config.prompt_desc: Edit persona and skill prompts directly in the browser.
config.appearance_desc: Choose the style, accent palette, and background atmosphere for your personal ARIA workspace.
help.title: ARIA Help
help.subtitle: ARIA uses the same wiki and help files locally as on GitHub, so the documentation only needs to be maintained once.
help.product_info_hint: Technical overview, feature list, architecture, and roadmap.
help.updates_hint: See the installed version, latest public version, and release notes.
help.doc_home_summary: Starting point for orientation, documentation paths, and recommended first steps.
help.doc_quick_start_summary: Fast path from Docker or Portainer to a usable ARIA instance.
help.doc_memory_summary: Memory, RAG documents, Memory Map, and recall behaviour explained compactly.
help.doc_skills_summary: How skills are structured, how triggers work, and how ARIA executes them.
help.doc_connections_summary: Overview of connection types, configuration, and routing value.
help.doc_releases_summary: How releases, local TAR updates, and upgrade flows fit together.
help.doc_pricing_summary: How ARIA resolves prices for LLM and embedding models and calculates costs.
help.doc_security_summary: Session, guardrail, and security principles of the current ALPHA line.
updates.title: ARIA Update Status
updates.subtitle: Checks GitHub tags and shows the release notes of the newest public version.
updates.manual_update_hint: Managed compose installs and internal aria-pull setups with an active helper can trigger the update directly below. Free-form Docker or Portainer paths intentionally stay manual.
updates.sequence_internal_body: 1) Provide the new internal TAR. 2) Run aria-pull on the target host. 3) Then quickly verify /stats and /updates.
updates.sequence_compose_body: 1) Use the controlled compose path via docker/setup-compose-stack.sh or an existing aria-stack.sh helper. 2) In the stack directory run ./aria-stack.sh update. 3) Then verify /stats, preflight and web search.
updates.sequence_portainer_body: Update the existing stack, keep volume and network names, and only add the delta you really need. Do not create a second empty instance on the side.
updates.sequence_gui_note_body: Managed compose installs and internal aria-pull setups can now trigger updates directly here. Free-form host or Portainer paths without a helper intentionally stay on the manual flow.
updates.notes_missing: No release notes were found for this version yet.
product_info.title: ARIA Product Info
product_info.subtitle: Read-only product, architecture, and release docs directly from the repo.
product_info.missing: No product documentation file found yet.
product_info.doc_overview_summary: What ARIA is, who it is for, and where the current ALPHA boundary is.
product_info.doc_feature_list_summary: Technical feature snapshot for chat, memory, skills, connections, UI, and deployment.
product_info.doc_architecture_summary: Layer model, routing, custom skills, persistence, security, and update strategy.
product_info.doc_copy_pack_summary: Short and long copy blocks for GitHub, release notes, landing pages, and external descriptions.
config.language_desc: Set the default language and edit language files in advanced mode.
config.connections_desc: Everything for external systems and access.
config.sample_connections_desc: These example profiles are shipped in /app/samples/connections and can be imported directly into config.yaml.
config.sample_connections_empty_desc: No samples/connections directory was found in the container.
config.workbench_desc: For debugging, fine-tuning, and “I know what I am doing” moments.
config.security_desc: Control bootstrap lock and login protection centrally.
config.logs_desc: Automatically clean up token/run logs and control retention.
config.files_desc: Direct access to relevant text files without an IDE.
config.error_interpreter_desc: Rules that translate technical SSH and package errors into clearer user messages.
config.backup_desc: Export your full ARIA configuration or restore it from a backup file.
config.backup_heading: Export or restore your ARIA configuration
config.backup_intro: Create a single backup file for the parts you configure inside ARIA, then restore it later if a release, container, or manual change goes sideways.
config.backup_contains_connections: connection profiles and routing metadata from config.yaml
config.backup_contains_connection_secret: connection passwords, tokens, and webhook URLs that live in the secure store
config.backup_contains_skills: custom skills and prompt markdown files
config.backup_excludes_memories: memories, Qdrant collections, and chat history
config.backup_excludes_runtime: runtime logs, uploads, update state, and Docker stack values
config.backup_excludes_ssh_keys: local SSH key files under data/ssh_keys are not exported; recreate or rotate them separately if needed
config.backup_excludes_note: Use this for configuration recovery, not for full data migration.
config.backup_export_desc: Download a JSON snapshot of your current ARIA configuration. Keep it somewhere safe.
config.backup_export_hint: Tip: create a fresh backup before larger upgrades or before importing a file from another instance.
config.backup_import_desc: Restore a previously exported backup file. ARIA will try to roll back to the previous state if the import fails.
config.backup_import_warn: Import replaces the active configuration snapshot, secure-store entries, and custom skill manifests with the contents of the backup file.
config_appearance.title: Appearance & theme
config_appearance.subtitle: Choose a calmer or bolder visual style for your personal ARIA workspace.
config_appearance.hint: The selection updates the whole interface and is stored centrally in the UI config.
config.admin_mode_off_desc: Advanced system areas are hidden. You can re-enable them under Users > Admin On/Off.
config.admin_mode_off_link_desc: There you can turn admin mode back on.
stats.cost_details_hint: Shows model usage and costs by source, for example chat, RSS, RAG, or memory.
stats.update_summary: A newer public release was found. Open the release notes.
stats.update_summary_current: No newer public release detected. Open the release status.
stats.routing_index_subtitle: Admin/debug status for the generated Qdrant routing collection. Not active in chat routing yet.
stats.routing_index_profiles_hint: Generated from SSH, SFTP, RSS, Discord and HTTP API connection metadata.
stats.health_subtitle: Lightweight runtime health of the main ARIA services.
stats.connections_live_title: Live status of all configured connections
stats.connections_live_hint: ARIA checks all configured connections here and refreshes the status when the page opens.
stats.connections_live_empty: No connections configured yet. As soon as profiles are saved, ARIA will show them here with live status.
stats.reset_subtitle: Deletes the stored token/cost/activity history. Memories, skills, and connection profiles stay untouched.
config_users.admin_toggle_effect: This controls advanced areas such as LLM, Embeddings, Security, Logs and the File Editor.
skills.readonly_mode: User mode is active. Skills can be viewed here, but changes are disabled.
skills.readonly_hint: In user mode, skills remain visible, but editing, importing and toggling are disabled.
base.restart_notice_text: ARIA was briefly unavailable. This page may be stale. Reload it if needed.

updates.cache_hint_text: If old colors or layouts still show up after a UI or CSS update: do a hard reload (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R). ARIA already versions static assets per release, but some browsers can still keep stale files around briefly.

context_help.llm_body: This is where you set API base, model, timeout, and limits for the chat brain. If answers get cut off or model loading fails, this page is the first place to check.
context_help.embeddings_body: Embeddings drive semantic search, memory recall, and RAG documents. If uploaded documents are not recalled well, this profile is worth checking first.
context_help.memory_body: Upload documents here, then manage them in the Memory Map. For larger knowledge sets, type and collection filters keep facts, rollups, and document chunks from blending together.
context_help.rss_body: Titles, short descriptions, aliases, and tags help ARIA route RSS-related prompts correctly. Feed groups are mainly a working view; good display names and keywords improve later chat matches.

login.cache_hint: If colors or layout still look old: do one hard reload.
updates.cache_hint_text: If old colors or layouts still show up after a UI or CSS update: do a hard reload (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R). ARIA already versions static assets per release, but some browsers can still keep stale files around briefly.
context_help.llm_body: This is where you set API base, model, timeout, and limits for the chat brain. If answers get cut off or model loading fails, this page is the first place to check.
context_help.embeddings_body: Embeddings drive semantic search, memory recall, and RAG documents. If uploaded documents are not recalled well, this profile is worth checking first.
context_help.memory_body: Upload documents here, then manage them in the Memory Map. For larger knowledge sets, type and collection filters keep facts, rollups, and document chunks from blending together.
context_help.rss_body: Titles, short descriptions, aliases, and tags help ARIA route RSS-related prompts correctly. Feed groups are mainly a working view; good display names and keywords improve later chat matches.
context_help.searxng_body: SearXNG remains its own service in the stack. ARIA only uses its JSON search API and later feeds the hits into chat with visible sources.
config_conn.searxng_subtitle: Self-hosted web search via the SearXNG JSON API.
config_conn.searxng_recommended_flow_tex: 1) Start SearXNG in the stack. 2) Create a profile for one search purpose. 3) ARIA tests the JSON search automatically. 4) Then use web search in chat.
config_conn.searxng_base_url_hint: ARIA uses the SearXNG JSON API at this base URL, for example http://searxng:8080.
config_conn.searxng_max_results_hint: How many hits should be pulled into chat context by default.
config_conn.searxng_fixed_base_url_hint: The SearXNG URL comes from the stack. Each profile only defines search behaviour and routing metadata.
config_conn.searxng_categories_hint: A few sensible categories are usually enough. If in doubt, keep only general enabled.
config_conn.searxng_engines_hint: This is where you define what the profile is for, for example YouTube for videos or Startpage for books.
config_conn.searxng_stack_missing_title: SearXNG in the stack is not reachable
config_conn.searxng_stack_missing_body: Start the SearXNG service in the stack first. As long as it is missing, connection tests and web search will fail.
config_conn.searxng_stack_warn_title: SearXNG responds, but should be checked
updates.run_started_body: ARIA is now starting the configured update path and refreshes this page while the run is active.
updates.run_requested_body: The update helper accepted the run. Please keep an eye on the status and log below.
updates.helper_disabled: This installation has no active GUI update helper.
updates.helper_disabled_body: This section only appears on installations with an active aria-updater helper.
updates.live_running_body: ARIA is currently recreating the running service. Keep this page open. It will reconnect automatically as soon as the health check is back.
updates.live_waiting_step: Waiting for ARIA to come back
updates.live_polling_hint: No manual reload needed. This page is polling the update helper and /health and will refresh itself when ARIA is reachable again.
updates.live_reconnected_hint: ARIA is reachable again. Reloading the update page now.
updates.live_waiting_hint: ARIA is still restarting. This page will keep waiting and reconnect automatically.
updates.live_unreachable_hint: ARIA is temporarily unreachable while the service is being recreated. Please keep this tab open.
updates.live_requested_hint: Update request accepted. Waiting for the helper to switch into the running state.
updates.live_submit_failed_hint: The request could not be sent cleanly. ARIA will keep trying to reconnect if the helper already started the update.
chat.tool_web_search_hint: Starts an explicit web search with sources.
chat.tool_open_stats_hint: Shows statistics and status pages directly from chat.
chat.tool_open_activities_hint: Opens Activities & Runs directly from chat.
chat.tool_update_run_hint: Starts the configured update path with a confirmation code.
chat.tool_update_status_hint: Queries the GUI update helper directly from chat.
chat.tool_backup_export_hint: Creates a download link for the current configuration backup.
chat.tool_backup_import_hint: Opens the restore path for an existing configuration backup.
